question.topic: Does AI improve creative output while reducing diversity?
question.primary_question: Does AI improve creative output while reducing diversity?
question.research_purpose: Understand whether AI-assisted ideation improves creative output while preserving diversity, and expose the evidence conditions that keep the graph from being overclaimed.
question.readiness_verdict: PARTIAL - fixture-backed graph is debug/frontend-readable; live arbitrary-source and automation readiness remain gated.

acquisition_parser_status.note: Full fixture graph sources were ingested into the mock-safe graph spine.
One abstract-first candidate had metadata but no accepted source text.
One candidate had TEI availability in acquisition audit metadata.
One candidate had PDF availability but parser readiness remained partial.

cluster.warning: Relationship density clears the fixture threshold, but this is not live arbitrary-source proof.

evidence_atoms.canonical_atom_text: AI-assisted brainstorming can increase rated idea diversity under explicit divergent-generation instructions.
evidence_atoms.why_clustered: Atom clusters 3 compatible claim(s) with shared scope/concepts.
evidence_atoms.why_weak: Fixture-backed seed evidence; not enough live-source diversity for automation.

evidence_atoms.canonical_atom_text: AI assistance may raise ideation throughput without guaranteeing originality gains in professional creative work.
evidence_atoms.why_clustered: Atom clusters 2 compatible claim(s) with shared scope/concepts.
evidence_atoms.why_weak: Originality transfer remains a caveat outside controlled fixture tasks.

evidence_atoms.canonical_atom_text: Semantic diversity trade-offs may be task-specific rather than universal across creative domains.
evidence_atoms.why_clustered: Atom clusters 3 compatible claim(s) with shared scope/concepts.
evidence_atoms.why_weak: The atom is useful for scoping, but still fixture-derived.

evidence_atoms.canonical_atom_text: Originality evidence remains sparse relative to diversity metrics in current fixture sources.
evidence_atoms.why_clustered: Atom remains a partial connection point for originality caveats.
evidence_atoms.why_weak: Sparse originality evidence keeps model/public readiness partial.

relationships.summary: AI assistance may reduce semantic diversity in short-form writing tasks.
relationships.explanation: Accepted quote-backed claims connect the same purpose-matched atom family to AI assistance and semantic diversity.

relationships.summary: Divergent prompting can increase rated diversity, creating a metric/condition contradiction with reduction claims.
relationships.explanation: The contradiction is preserved as an apparent metric-or-condition difference, not flattened into a generic mixed-effect summary.

relationships.summary: Prompting and human-control conditions qualify whether diversity loss appears.
relationships.explanation: Qualification edges show why readiness stays partial for local model or public claims.

relationships.summary: Semantic diversity is treated as a measured dimension of diversity.
relationships.explanation: Definition edges make the concept path legible before a graph visualization exists.

relationships.summary: Writing-task diversity and broader creative diversity differ by scope.
relationships.explanation: Scope-difference edges keep fixture findings from being generalized beyond the contract.

trace_details.claim_summary: AI-assisted brainstorming can increase rated idea diversity under explicit divergent-generation instructions.
trace_details.public_safe_connection_explanation: Accepted quote-backed claim links source provenance to concepts, a support relationship, and the fixture cluster without exposing raw quote text or private row IDs.

trace_details.claim_summary: Originality evidence remains sparse relative to diversity metrics in current fixture sources.
trace_details.public_safe_connection_explanation: The trace shows a weak originality path that explains readiness gaps while keeping raw quotes, local paths, raw prompt fields, and private diagnostics out of the preview.

local_model.reason: Purpose gates and typed fixture traces are in place, but arbitrary live Ollama source runs are not proven.

paid_api.reason: No paid provider, cloud model, or escalation route is wired.

frontend_public_display.reason: The static preview is safe and useful for debugging; public claims must still stay fixture-labeled.

automation.reason: Acquisition/parser failures and live-source purpose enforcement remain blockers.

top_blockers: Purpose gates are not yet enforced across every future live arbitrary-source path.
Relationship density is fixture-proven, not arbitrary live research proof.
Acquisition status/parser metadata is not consistently persisted into source metadata for the full fixture graph path.
Paid API and automation readiness remain NO-GO.
graph_health_warnings: Weak/partial readiness is visible even though fixture relationship density clears the 0.5 threshold.
Source health includes blocked quality-gate, parse failure, dirty webpage, and unavailable PDF parser reasons.
gaps_next_move.recommender_reason: The current graph is understandable from fixture data; the next risk is preventing purpose-mismatched or parser-weak live evidence from entering later graph stages.
